# 40 acres app Website Audit

Website: https://www.40acresapp.com/
Company: 40 acres app
Contact: dANIEL Easterly
Email: info@40acrersapp.com
Audit Date: June 23, 2026
Audited By: Codex

---

## Audit Summary

40 acres app's website was reviewed from a public-facing perspective.

Main opportunities:

1. Improve speed and reduce what loads before the first view
2. Make the message, hierarchy, and CTA path easier to scan
3. Clean up code and third-party scripts that are not needed right away
4. Optimise images and other visual assets
5. Improve search structure, page titles, and heading quality
6. Clean up accessibility and public-facing quality details

---

## Messaging Direction

Recommended messaging model: Platform-Led

Messaging Notes:

Based on the public page text and navigation, 40 acres app appears to include multiple audiences, services, features, or use cases. The website should group those pieces into a clearer complete solution with direct next steps.

---

## Performance

### Speed Test Scores

Performance: 59
Accessibility: 95
Best Practices: 73
SEO: 100

### Main Performance Issues

[1] Performance score is below the recommended target.
[2] The main visible content takes too long to load.
[6] The page is loading too much before the first view is usable.
[8] The total page weight is too high.
[9] The page feels slower than it should for the amount of content shown.

### The Affects

Load speed
Mobile experience
First impression
Bounce rate
Conversion
Speed test score
Page usability
Perceived site quality

### The Fixes

Prioritise the main above-the-fold content.
Optimise the main loading media.
Reduce code and styling that blocks the first view.
Delay non-essential scripts until after the page is usable.
Compress and convert large media assets.
Improve loading order so the main content renders first.

---

## Style & Site Experience

### Main Issues

[3] The heading structure makes the page harder to scan.

### The Affects

Clarity
First impression
User flow
Conversion
Perceived quality
Professional finish
Readability
Visitor attention
CTA engagement

### The Fixes

Improve visual hierarchy.
Strengthen the main CTA path.
Improve spacing and page rhythm.
Make the site feel more focused and easier to scan.
Make navigation and footer links clearer.

---

## Animation & Interactions

### Main Issues

[7] Visual media is costing too much performance.

### The Affects

Page speed
Mobile experience
Speed performance
User attention
Conversion flow
Interaction quality

### The Fixes

Move non-essential animation code behind page load.
Prioritise content loading before decorative animation.
Reduce movement that distracts from the main message.
Make sure interactions support the user journey.
Review visual effects for performance cost.

---

## Code & Third-Party Scripts

### Main Issues

[1] Heavy code is slowing the page down.
[3] Unused scripts are being loaded.
[8] Console errors are visible.

### The Affects

Load speed
Speed test score
User interaction delay
Tracking reliability
First view
Mobile performance

### The Fixes

Audit all code loaded on the page.
Remove unused scripts and duplicate libraries.
Defer non-critical code.
Move non-essential scripts behind page load.
Load third-party scripts after the main content has rendered.
Reduce the number of scripts needed for the first view.

---

## Asset & Media Performance

### Main Issues

[1] Large images are increasing page weight.
[11] Images are larger than they need to be.
[12] Media is not compressed enough.
[13] Too many visual assets are loading on the homepage.
[14] The page relies on heavy above-the-fold media.

### The Affects

Total page size
Main content loading speed
Mobile performance
First impression
Bandwidth usage
Speed performance
First view rendering

### The Fixes

Compress large media assets.
Prioritise the main image or visual.
Resize images to match their display size.
Rework heavy above-the-fold media if it delays page render.
Lazy-load below-the-fold media.
Make sure critical visuals load before non-critical media.

---

## SEO & Crawlability

### Main Issues

[3] Heading structure is unclear.
[4] The page has multiple main headings.
[6] Supporting headings are not structured properly.

### The Affects

Google discovery
Indexing
Search snippets
Crawl efficiency
Content quality signals
Organic performance
Page relevance
Site structure

### The Fixes

Clean up page titles and meta descriptions.
Use one clear main heading per page.
Structure supporting headings properly.
Improve internal linking to key pages.
Strengthen page focus around the most valuable search intent.
Make sure product or service content is visible to search engines.

---

## Website Standards

### Main Issues

[3] Console errors are visible.
[18] Accessibility basics need improvement.

### The Affects

Accessibility
Usability
Professional finish
Conversion
Navigation clarity
Public-facing quality
Visitor confidence

### The Fixes

Add clear labels to image-based buttons and links.
Check colour contrast on key text and buttons.
Make buttons and links clearer.
Improve navigation and footer structure.
Improve accessibility basics.
Make the site feel more complete and professionally finished.

# Aldin AI Website Audit

Website: https://aldin.ai/
Company: Aldin AI
Contact: Salah Mokhayesh
Email: salah@aldin.ai
Audit Date: June 23, 2026
Audited By: Codex

---

## Audit Summary

Aldin AI's website was reviewed from a public-facing perspective.

Main opportunities:

1. Improve speed and reduce what loads before the first view
2. Clean up code and third-party scripts that are not needed right away
3. Optimise images, video, and other visual assets

---

## Messaging Direction

Recommended messaging model: Product-Led

Messaging Notes:

Based on the public page text, Aldin AI should make the product clear quickly: what it does, who it is for, and what the visitor should do next.

---

## Performance

### Speed Test Scores

Performance: 93
Accessibility: 100
Best Practices: 100
SEO: 100

### Main Performance Issues

[6] The page is loading too much before the first view is usable.
[7] Code or styling is delaying the first view of the page.
[8] The total page weight is too high.

### The Affects

Load speed
Mobile experience
First impression
Bounce rate
Conversion
Speed test score
Page usability
Perceived site quality

### The Fixes

Prioritise the main above-the-fold content.
Optimise the main loading media.
Reduce code and styling that blocks the first view.
Delay non-essential scripts until after the page is usable.
Compress and convert large media assets.
Improve loading order so the main content renders first.

---

## Style & Site Experience

### Main Issues

No major issue was found in this section from the external checks.

### The Affects

No specific effect selected from the external checks.

### The Fixes

No specific fix selected from the external checks.

---

## Animation & Interactions

### Main Issues

[5] Heavy above-the-fold media is loading before the main content is fully usable.

### The Affects

Page speed
Mobile experience
Speed performance
User attention
Conversion flow
Interaction quality

### The Fixes

Move non-essential animation code behind page load.
Prioritise content loading before decorative animation.
Reduce movement that distracts from the main message.
Make sure interactions support the user journey.
Review visual effects for performance cost.

---

## Code & Third-Party Scripts

### Main Issues

[1] Heavy code is slowing the page down.
[2] Too much code is running before page load.
[3] Unused scripts are being loaded.
[11] Code or styling is blocking the first view of the page.

### The Affects

Load speed
Speed test score
User interaction delay
Tracking reliability
First view
Mobile performance

### The Fixes

Audit all code loaded on the page.
Remove unused scripts and duplicate libraries.
Defer non-critical code.
Move non-essential scripts behind page load.
Load third-party scripts after the main content has rendered.
Reduce the number of scripts needed for the first view.

---

## Asset & Media Performance

### Main Issues

[1] Large images are increasing page weight.
[2] Large videos are increasing page weight.
[11] Images are larger than they need to be.
[12] Media is not compressed enough.
[10] Background media is delaying the first view.

### The Affects

Total page size
Main content loading speed
Mobile performance
First impression
Bandwidth usage
Speed performance
First view rendering

### The Fixes

Compress large media assets.
Prioritise the main image or visual.
Resize images to match their display size.
Rework heavy above-the-fold media if it delays page render.
Lazy-load below-the-fold media.
Make sure critical visuals load before non-critical media.

---

audits.sourceText:
# Conference Captioning Website Audit

Website: https://conferencecaptioning.com/
Company: Conference Captioning
Contact: Saamer Mansoor
Email: hello@conferencecaptioning.com
Audit Date: June 23, 2026
Audited By: Codex

---

## Audit Summary

Conference Captioning's website was reviewed from a public-facing perspective.

Main opportunities:

1. Improve speed and reduce what loads before the first view
2. Make the message, hierarchy, and CTA path easier to scan
3. Clean up code and third-party scripts that are not needed right away
4. Optimise images, video, and other visual assets
5. Improve search structure, page titles, and heading quality
6. Clean up accessibility and public-facing quality details

---

## Messaging Direction

Recommended messaging model: Platform-Led

Messaging Notes:

Based on the public page text and navigation, Conference Captioning appears to include multiple audiences, services, features, or use cases. The website should group those pieces into a clearer complete solution with direct next steps.

---

## Performance

### Speed Test Scores

Performance: 67
Accessibility: 97
Best Practices: 96
SEO: 100

### Main Performance Issues

[1] Performance score is below the recommended target.
[2] The main visible content takes too long to load.
[7] Code or styling is delaying the first view of the page.
[9] The page feels slower than it should for the amount of content shown.

### The Affects

Load speed
Mobile experience
First impression
Bounce rate
Conversion
Speed test score
Page usability
Perceived site quality

### The Fixes

Prioritise the main above-the-fold content.
Optimise the main loading media.
Reduce code and styling that blocks the first view.
Delay non-essential scripts until after the page is usable.
Compress and convert large media assets.
Improve loading order so the main content renders first.

---

## Style & Site Experience

### Main Issues

[3] The heading structure makes the page harder to scan.
[13] The page structure should be simplified so it is easier to scan.

### The Affects

Clarity
First impression
User flow
Conversion
Perceived quality
Professional finish
Readability
Visitor attention
CTA engagement

### The Fixes

Improve visual hierarchy.
Strengthen the main CTA path.
Improve spacing and page rhythm.
Make the site feel more focused and easier to scan.
Make navigation and footer links clearer.

---

## Animation & Interactions

### Main Issues

[5] Heavy above-the-fold media is loading before the main content is fully usable.
[7] Visual media is costing too much performance.

### The Affects

Page speed
Mobile experience
Speed performance
User attention
Conversion flow
Interaction quality

### The Fixes

Move non-essential animation code behind page load.
Prioritise content loading before decorative animation.
Reduce movement that distracts from the main message.
Make sure interactions support the user journey.
Review visual effects for performance cost.

---

## Code & Third-Party Scripts

### Main Issues

[1] Heavy code is slowing the page down.
[2] Too much code is running before page load.
[3] Unused scripts are being loaded.
[8] Console errors are visible.
[11] Code or styling is blocking the first view of the page.

### The Affects

Load speed
Speed test score
User interaction delay
Tracking reliability
First view
Mobile performance

### The Fixes

Audit all code loaded on the page.
Remove unused scripts and duplicate libraries.
Defer non-critical code.
Move non-essential scripts behind page load.
Load third-party scripts after the main content has rendered.
Reduce the number of scripts needed for the first view.

---

## Asset & Media Performance

### Main Issues

[1] Large images are increasing page weight.
[2] Large videos are increasing page weight.
[11] Images are larger than they need to be.
[12] Media is not compressed enough.
[10] Background media is delaying the first view.
[14] The page relies on heavy above-the-fold media.

### The Affects

Total page size
Main content loading speed
Mobile performance
First impression
Bandwidth usage
Speed performance
First view rendering

### The Fixes

Compress large media assets.
Prioritise the main image or visual.
Resize images to match their display size.
Rework heavy above-the-fold media if it delays page render.
Lazy-load below-the-fold media.
Make sure critical visuals load before non-critical media.

---

## SEO & Crawlability

### Main Issues

[3] Heading structure is unclear.
[4] The page has multiple main headings.

### The Affects

Google discovery
Indexing
Search snippets
Crawl efficiency
Content quality signals
Organic performance
Page relevance
Site structure

### The Fixes

Clean up page titles and meta descriptions.
Use one clear main heading per page.
Structure supporting headings properly.
Improve internal linking to key pages.
Strengthen page focus around the most valuable search intent.
Make sure product or service content is visible to search engines.

---

## Website Standards

### Main Issues

[9] Page structure is harder to scan than it should be.
[13] Navigation and link structure should be simplified.
[3] Console errors are visible.

### The Affects

Accessibility
Usability
Professional finish
Conversion
Navigation clarity
Public-facing quality
Visitor confidence

### The Fixes

Add clear labels to image-based buttons and links.
Check colour contrast on key text and buttons.
Make buttons and links clearer.
Improve navigation and footer structure.
Improve accessibility basics.
Make the site feel more complete and professionally finished.

audits.sourceText:
# DMR International Inc. Website Audit

Website: https://dmri-inc.com/
Company: DMR International Inc.
Contact: Rick Lastella
Email: ricklatella@dmri-inc.com
Audit Date: June 23, 2026
Audited By: Codex

---

## Audit Summary

DMR International Inc.'s website was reviewed from a public-facing perspective.

Main opportunities:

1. Improve speed and reduce what loads before the first view
2. Make the message, hierarchy, and CTA path easier to scan
3. Clean up code and third-party scripts that are not needed right away
4. Optimise images, video, and other visual assets
5. Improve search structure, page titles, and heading quality
6. Clean up accessibility and public-facing quality details

---

## Messaging Direction

Recommended messaging model: Platform-Led

Messaging Notes:

Based on the public page text and navigation, DMR International Inc. appears to include multiple audiences, services, features, or use cases. The website should group those pieces into a clearer complete solution with direct next steps.

---

## Performance

### Speed Test Scores

Performance: 57
Accessibility: 87
Best Practices: 73
SEO: 100

### Main Performance Issues

[1] Performance score is below the recommended target.
[2] The main visible content takes too long to load.
[3] The first part of the page takes too long to appear.
[6] The page is loading too much before the first view is usable.
[7] Code or styling is delaying the first view of the page.
[8] The total page weight is too high.
[9] The page feels slower than it should for the amount of content shown.

### The Affects

Load speed
Mobile experience
First impression
Bounce rate
Conversion
Speed test score
Page usability
Perceived site quality

### The Fixes

Prioritise the main above-the-fold content.
Optimise the main loading media.
Reduce code and styling that blocks the first view.
Delay non-essential scripts until after the page is usable.
Compress and convert large media assets.
Improve loading order so the main content renders first.

---

## Style & Site Experience

### Main Issues

[3] The heading structure makes the page harder to scan.
[9] Some links or buttons are not clearly labelled.

### The Affects

Clarity
First impression
User flow
Conversion
Perceived quality
Professional finish
Readability
Visitor attention
CTA engagement

### The Fixes

Improve visual hierarchy.
Strengthen the main CTA path.
Improve spacing and page rhythm.
Make the site feel more focused and easier to scan.
Make navigation and footer links clearer.

---

## Animation & Interactions

### Main Issues

[5] Heavy above-the-fold media is loading before the main content is fully usable.
[7] Visual media is costing too much performance.
[6] Interactions may feel slower because too much code is loading.

### The Affects

Page speed
Mobile experience
Speed performance
User attention
Conversion flow
Interaction quality

### The Fixes

Move non-essential animation code behind page load.
Prioritise content loading before decorative animation.
Reduce movement that distracts from the main message.
Make sure interactions support the user journey.
Review visual effects for performance cost.

---

## Code & Third-Party Scripts

### Main Issues

[1] Heavy code is slowing the page down.
[2] Too much code is running before page load.
[3] Unused scripts are being loaded.
[8] Console errors are visible.
[11] Code or styling is blocking the first view of the page.

### The Affects

Load speed
Speed test score
User interaction delay
Tracking reliability
First view
Mobile performance

### The Fixes

Audit all code loaded on the page.
Remove unused scripts and duplicate libraries.
Defer non-critical code.
Move non-essential scripts behind page load.
Load third-party scripts after the main content has rendered.
Reduce the number of scripts needed for the first view.

---

## Asset & Media Performance

### Main Issues

[1] Large images are increasing page weight.
[2] Large videos are increasing page weight.
[11] Images are larger than they need to be.
[12] Media is not compressed enough.
[10] Background media is delaying the first view.
[14] The page relies on heavy above-the-fold media.

### The Affects

Total page size
Main content loading speed
Mobile performance
First impression
Bandwidth usage
Speed performance
First view rendering

### The Fixes

Compress large media assets.
Prioritise the main image or visual.
Resize images to match their display size.
Rework heavy above-the-fold media if it delays page render.
Lazy-load below-the-fold media.
Make sure critical visuals load before non-critical media.

---

## SEO & Crawlability

### Main Issues

[1] Meta titles are weak or too generic.
[3] Heading structure is unclear.
[5] The page is missing a clear main heading.

### The Affects

Google discovery
Indexing
Search snippets
Crawl efficiency
Content quality signals
Organic performance
Page relevance
Site structure

### The Fixes

Clean up page titles and meta descriptions.
Use one clear main heading per page.
Structure supporting headings properly.
Improve internal linking to key pages.
Strengthen page focus around the most valuable search intent.
Make sure product or service content is visible to search engines.

---

## Website Standards

### Main Issues

[5] Some buttons or image links do not have clear labels.
[6] Some form labels or accessibility details need review.
[8] Colour contrast is too low in some areas.

[18] Accessibility basics need improvement.

Improve accessibility basics.
Make the site feel more complete and professionally finished.

# MeetMatch.ai Website Audit

Website: https://meetmatch.ai
Company: MeetMatch.ai
Contact: Bretton Badenoch
Email: bretton@meetmatch.ai
Audit Date: June 23, 2026
Audited By: Codex

---

## Audit Summary

MeetMatch.ai's website was reviewed from a public-facing perspective.

Main opportunities:

1. Improve speed and reduce what loads before the first view
2. Clean up code and third-party scripts that are not needed right away
3. Optimise images, video, and other visual assets
4. Clean up accessibility and public-facing quality details

---

## Messaging Direction

Recommended messaging model: Platform-Led

Messaging Notes:

Based on the public page text and navigation, MeetMatch.ai appears to include multiple audiences, services, features, or use cases. The website should group those pieces into a clearer complete solution with direct next steps.

---

## Performance

### Speed Test Scores

Performance: 62
Accessibility: 96
Best Practices: 100
SEO: 100

### Main Performance Issues

[1] Performance score is below the recommended target.
[2] The main visible content takes too long to load.
[3] The first part of the page takes too long to appear.
[7] Code or styling is delaying the first view of the page.
[9] The page feels slower than it should for the amount of content shown.

### The Affects

Load speed
Mobile experience
First impression
Bounce rate
Conversion
Speed test score
Page usability
Perceived site quality

### The Fixes

Prioritise the main above-the-fold content.
Optimise the main loading media.
Reduce code and styling that blocks the first view.
Delay non-essential scripts until after the page is usable.
Compress and convert large media assets.
Improve loading order so the main content renders first.

---

## Style & Site Experience

### Main Issues

No major issue was found in this section from the external checks.

### The Affects

No specific effect selected from the external checks.

### The Fixes

No specific fix selected from the external checks.

---

## Animation & Interactions

### Main Issues

[5] Heavy above-the-fold media is loading before the main content is fully usable.
[7] Visual media is costing too much performance.

### The Affects

Page speed
Mobile experience
Speed performance
User attention
Conversion flow
Interaction quality

### The Fixes

Move non-essential animation code behind page load.
Prioritise content loading before decorative animation.
Reduce movement that distracts from the main message.
Make sure interactions support the user journey.
Review visual effects for performance cost.

---

## Code & Third-Party Scripts

### Main Issues

[1] Heavy code is slowing the page down.
[2] Too much code is running before page load.
[3] Unused scripts are being loaded.
[11] Code or styling is blocking the first view of the page.

### The Affects

Load speed
Speed test score
User interaction delay
Tracking reliability
First view
Mobile performance

### The Fixes

Audit all code loaded on the page.
Remove unused scripts and duplicate libraries.
Defer non-critical code.
Move non-essential scripts behind page load.
Load third-party scripts after the main content has rendered.
Reduce the number of scripts needed for the first view.

---

## Asset & Media Performance

### Main Issues

[1] Large images are increasing page weight.
[2] Large videos are increasing page weight.
[11] Images are larger than they need to be.
[12] Media is not compressed enough.
[10] Background media is delaying the first view.
[14] The page relies on heavy above-the-fold media.

### The Affects

Total page size
Main content loading speed
Mobile performance
First impression
Bandwidth usage
Speed performance
First view rendering

### The Fixes

Compress large media assets.
Prioritise the main image or visual.
Resize images to match their display size.
Rework heavy above-the-fold media if it delays page render.
Lazy-load below-the-fold media.
Make sure critical visuals load before non-critical media.

---

## SEO & Crawlability

### Main Issues

No major issue was found in this section from the external checks.

### The Affects

No specific effect selected from the external checks.

### The Fixes

No specific fix selected from the external checks.

---

## Website Standards

### Main Issues

[8] Colour contrast is too low in some areas.
[18] Accessibility basics need improvement.

### The Affects

Accessibility
Usability
Professional finish
Conversion
Navigation clarity
Public-facing quality
Visitor confidence

### The Fixes

Add clear labels to image-based buttons and links.
Check colour contrast on key text and buttons.
Make buttons and links clearer.
Improve navigation and footer structure.
Improve accessibility basics.
Make the site feel more complete and professionally finished.

Audit Date: June 23, 2026

---

## Audit Summary

ScoreNest's website was reviewed from a public-facing perspective.

Main opportunities:

1. Improve speed and reduce what loads before the first view

6. Clean up accessibility and public-facing quality details

---

Accessibility: 98

Speed test score

# Think Technologies Website Audit

Website: https://www.think-technologies.com/
Company: Think Technologies
Contact: Shaun Zhang
Email: zealotrobert@gmail.com
Audit Date: June 23, 2026
Audited By: Codex

---

## Audit Summary

Think Technologies's website was reviewed from a public-facing perspective.

Main opportunities:

1. Improve speed and reduce what loads before the first view
2. Make the message, hierarchy, and CTA path easier to scan
3. Clean up code and third-party scripts that are not needed right away
4. Optimise images, video, and other visual assets
5. Improve search structure, page titles, and heading quality
6. Clean up accessibility and public-facing quality details

---

## Messaging Direction

Recommended messaging model: Platform-Led

Messaging Notes:

Based on the public page text and navigation, Think Technologies appears to include multiple audiences, services, features, or use cases. The website should group those pieces into a clearer complete solution with direct next steps.

---

## Performance

### Speed Test Scores

Performance: 56
Accessibility: 95
Best Practices: 77
SEO: 92

### Main Performance Issues

[1] Performance score is below the recommended target.
[2] The main visible content takes too long to load.
[3] The first part of the page takes too long to appear.
[6] The page is loading too much before the first view is usable.
[7] Code or styling is delaying the first view of the page.
[8] The total page weight is too high.
[9] The page feels slower than it should for the amount of content shown.

### The Affects

Load speed
Mobile experience
First impression
Bounce rate
Conversion
Speed test score
Page usability
Perceived site quality

### The Fixes

Prioritise the main above-the-fold content.
Optimise the main loading media.
Reduce code and styling that blocks the first view.
Delay non-essential scripts until after the page is usable.
Compress and convert large media assets.
Improve loading order so the main content renders first.

---

## Style & Site Experience

### Main Issues

[3] The heading structure makes the page harder to scan.
[9] Some links or buttons are not clearly labelled.

### The Affects

Clarity
First impression
User flow
Conversion
Perceived quality
Professional finish
Readability
Visitor attention
CTA engagement

### The Fixes

Improve visual hierarchy.
Strengthen the main CTA path.
Improve spacing and page rhythm.
Make the site feel more focused and easier to scan.
Make navigation and footer links clearer.

---

## Animation & Interactions

### Main Issues

[5] Heavy above-the-fold media is loading before the main content is fully usable.
[7] Visual media is costing too much performance.

### The Affects

Page speed
Mobile experience
Speed performance
User attention
Conversion flow
Interaction quality

### The Fixes

Move non-essential animation code behind page load.
Prioritise content loading before decorative animation.
Reduce movement that distracts from the main message.
Make sure interactions support the user journey.
Review visual effects for performance cost.

---

## Code & Third-Party Scripts

### Main Issues

[1] Heavy code is slowing the page down.
[2] Too much code is running before page load.
[3] Unused scripts are being loaded.
[11] Code or styling is blocking the first view of the page.

### The Affects

Load speed
Speed test score
User interaction delay
Tracking reliability
First view
Mobile performance

### The Fixes

Audit all code loaded on the page.
Remove unused scripts and duplicate libraries.
Defer non-critical code.
Move non-essential scripts behind page load.
Load third-party scripts after the main content has rendered.
Reduce the number of scripts needed for the first view.

---

## Asset & Media Performance

### Main Issues

[1] Large images are increasing page weight.
[2] Large videos are increasing page weight.
[11] Images are larger than they need to be.
[12] Media is not compressed enough.
[10] Background media is delaying the first view.
[13] Too many visual assets are loading on the homepage.
[14] The page relies on heavy above-the-fold media.

### The Affects

Total page size
Main content loading speed
Mobile performance
First impression
Bandwidth usage
Speed performance
First view rendering

### The Fixes

Compress large media assets.
Prioritise the main image or visual.
Resize images to match their display size.
Rework heavy above-the-fold media if it delays page render.
Lazy-load below-the-fold media.
Make sure critical visuals load before non-critical media.

[3] Heading structure is unclear.

---

[18] Accessibility basics need improvement.

### The Affects

Usability

Make the site feel more complete and professionally finished.

# wheel.me Website Audit

Website: https://wheel.me
Company: wheel.me
Contact: Rasmus Bendvold
Email: rasmus@wheel.me
Audit Date: June 23, 2026
Audited By: Codex

---

## Audit Summary

wheel.me's website was reviewed from a public-facing perspective.

Main opportunities:

1. Improve speed and reduce what loads before the first view
2. Make the message, hierarchy, and CTA path easier to scan
3. Clean up code and third-party scripts that are not needed right away
4. Optimise images, video, and other visual assets
5. Improve search structure, page titles, and heading quality
6. Clean up accessibility and public-facing quality details

---

## Messaging Direction

Recommended messaging model: Platform-Led

Messaging Notes:

Based on the public page text and navigation, wheel.me appears to include multiple audiences, services, features, or use cases. The website should group those pieces into a clearer complete solution with direct next steps.

---

## Performance

### Speed Test Scores

Performance: 60
Accessibility: 85
Best Practices: 73
SEO: 82

### Main Performance Issues

[1] Performance score is below the recommended target.
[2] The main visible content takes too long to load.
[3] The first part of the page takes too long to appear.
[6] The page is loading too much before the first view is usable.
[7] Code or styling is delaying the first view of the page.
[8] The total page weight is too high.
[9] The page feels slower than it should for the amount of content shown.

### The Affects

Load speed
Mobile experience
First impression
Bounce rate
Conversion
Speed test score
Page usability
Perceived site quality

### The Fixes

Prioritise the main above-the-fold content.
Optimise the main loading media.
Reduce code and styling that blocks the first view.
Delay non-essential scripts until after the page is usable.
Compress and convert large media assets.
Improve loading order so the main content renders first.

---

## Style & Site Experience

### Main Issues

[2] The page title or description does not explain the site clearly enough.
[3] The heading structure makes the page harder to scan.
[9] Some links or buttons are not clearly labelled.
[13] The page structure should be simplified so it is easier to scan.

### The Affects

Clarity
First impression
User flow
Conversion
Perceived quality
Professional finish
Readability
Visitor attention
CTA engagement

### The Fixes

Improve visual hierarchy.
Strengthen the main CTA path.
Improve spacing and page rhythm.
Make the site feel more focused and easier to scan.
Make navigation and footer links clearer.

---

## Animation & Interactions

### Main Issues

[5] Heavy above-the-fold media is loading before the main content is fully usable.
[7] Visual media is costing too much performance.

### The Affects

Page speed
Mobile experience
Speed performance
User attention
Conversion flow
Interaction quality

### The Fixes

Move non-essential animation code behind page load.
Prioritise content loading before decorative animation.
Reduce movement that distracts from the main message.
Make sure interactions support the user journey.
Review visual effects for performance cost.

---

## Code & Third-Party Scripts

### Main Issues

[1] Heavy code is slowing the page down.
[2] Too much code is running before page load.
[3] Unused scripts are being loaded.
[8] Console errors are visible.
[11] Code or styling is blocking the first view of the page.

### The Affects

Load speed
Speed test score
User interaction delay
Tracking reliability
First view
Mobile performance

### The Fixes

Audit all code loaded on the page.
Remove unused scripts and duplicate libraries.
Defer non-critical code.
Move non-essential scripts behind page load.
Load third-party scripts after the main content has rendered.
Reduce the number of scripts needed for the first view.

---

## Asset & Media Performance

### Main Issues

[1] Large images are increasing page weight.
[2] Large videos are increasing page weight.
[11] Images are larger than they need to be.
[12] Media is not compressed enough.
[10] Background media is delaying the first view.
[13] Too many visual assets are loading on the homepage.
[14] The page relies on heavy above-the-fold media.

### The Affects

Total page size
Main content loading speed
Mobile performance
First impression
Bandwidth usage
Speed performance
First view rendering

### The Fixes

Compress large media assets.
Prioritise the main image or visual.
Resize images to match their display size.
Rework heavy above-the-fold media if it delays page render.
Lazy-load below-the-fold media.
Make sure critical visuals load before non-critical media.

---

[1] Meta titles are weak or too generic.
[2] Meta descriptions are missing or too thin.

Make sure product or service content is visible to search engines.

---

Make the site feel more complete and professionally finished.
